questions.prompt: Why shouldn't you use readFileSync in a production HTTP server?
options: It blocks the event loop until the file is read — no other requests are served
It only works in CJS modules
questions.explanation: Synchronous I/O blocks the event loop. While reading, all other connections wait. Use fs/promises or callbacks instead.
TypeScript types all process.env values as string | undefined because they may not be set.

questions.prompt: How do you get __dirname in an ES module Node.js file?
options: __dirname is available in ESM just like in CJS
Derive it from import.meta.url using fileURLToPath and path.dirname
questions.explanation: __dirname doesn't exist in ESM. You derive it: const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

questions.prompt: In a raw Node.js HTTP server, how does the request body arrive?
options: As a parsed object in req.body
As a stream of chunks via 'data' events
questions.explanation: The body is a readable stream. You must collect 'data' events and assemble the full body, then parse it.

questions.prompt: What happens if you never call res.end() in a Node.js HTTP handler?
options: Node.js automatically closes the response after 30 seconds
The connection hangs until the client times out
The response sends with empty body automatically
questions.explanation: You must always call res.end(). Failing to do so keeps the connection open until the client or server times out.

questions.prompt: Write a Node.js HTTP handler that returns a 200 JSON response { ok: true }.
Which signal should a Node.js server listen for to handle graceful shutdown in a container?
questions.explanation: Containers send SIGTERM before stopping. SIGKILL cannot be caught. Handling SIGTERM lets you close the server cleanly.
